sanskrit:
तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान्।
क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु॥
transliteration:
tān ahaṁ dviṣataḥ krūrān saṁsāreṣu narādhamān
kṣipāmy ajasram aśubhān āsurīṣv eva yoniṣu
literal_meaning: Those—envious, cruel, lowest among humans—I hurl into the cycles of saṁsāra, into demoniac wombs again and again.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes how chronic envy and cruelty crystallize into a self-reinforcing trajectory: the mind that refuses to acknowledge interdependence is propelled into life-situations that mirror its own hostility, locking it into patterns of escalating alienation.
psychological_state: Moral dread—Arjuna confronts the possibility that persistent malice becomes a self-selected prison of ever-dehumanizing roles.
trigger_scenario: Reading the comments under a viral post and realizing how quickly contempt becomes identity, trapping people in echo-chambers of outrage.
anchor_text: Hate isolates you; compassion reconnects you to life.
